verses.2: “I have heard many things like these; Miserable comforters are you all!
verses.3: “Is there no end to windy words? Or what provokes you that you answer?
verses.4: “I too could speak like you, If only I were in your place. I could compose words against you And shake my head at you.
verses.5: “ Or I could strengthen you with my mouth, And the condolence of my lips could lessen your pain.
verses.6: “If I speak, my pain is not lessened, And if I refrain, what pain leaves me?
verses.7: “But now He has exhausted me; You have laid waste all my group of loved ones.
verses.8: “And you have shriveled me up, It has become a witness; And my infirmity rises up against me, It testifies to my face.
verses.9: “His anger has torn me and hunted me down, He has gnashed at me with His teeth; My enemy glares at me.
verses.10: “They have gaped at me with their mouths, They have slapped me on the cheek with contempt; They have massed themselves against me.
verses.11: “God hands me over to criminals, And tosses me into the hands of the wicked.
verses.12: “I was at ease, but He shattered me, And He has grasped me by my neck and shaken me to pieces; He has also set me up as His target.
verses.13: “His arrows surround me. He splits my kidneys open without mercy; He pours out my bile on the ground.
verses.14: “He breaks through me with breach after breach; He runs at me like a warrior.
verses.15: “I have sewed sackcloth over my skin, And thrust my horn in the dust.
verses.16: “My face is flushed from weeping, And deep darkness is on my eyelids,
verses.17: Although there is no violence in my hands, And my prayer is pure.
verses.18: “Earth, do not cover my blood, And may there be no resting place for my cry.
verses.19: “Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven, And my advocate is on high.
verses.20: “My friends are my scoffers; My eye weeps to God,
verses.21: That one might plead for a man with God As a son of man with his neighbor!
verses.22: “For when a few years are past, I shall go the way of no return.
